name: Java
description: A robust, object-oriented language used in enterprise software, Android development, and large-scale systems.

lessons.title: Hello World & Main Method
lessons.content: Every Java program starts with a class containing a main method. The System.out.println() method prints output followed by a newline. Java is statically typed — every variable must have a declared type.

exercises.question: What is the entry point of every Java program?
hints: Its full signature is: public static void main(String[] args)

exercises.question: Which method prints output to the console in Java?
hints: It belongs to the System.out stream

lessons.title: Data Types & Variables
lessons.content: Java is strongly typed. Primitive types: int, double, boolean, char. Reference types include String and all objects. Variables must be declared with their type before use.

exercises.question: Which Java type stores a whole number?
hints: Short for integer — no decimal point

exercises.question: How is a String literal written in Java?
hints: Java uses double quotes for String literals

lessons.title: Classes & Objects
lessons.content: Java is object-oriented. A class is a blueprint; an object is an instance. Classes have fields (data) and methods (behaviour). The 'new' keyword creates an object.

exercises.question: What keyword creates a new object from a class?

lessons.title: Inheritance & Polymorphism
lessons.content: Inheritance lets a class extend another class, reusing its fields and methods. The 'extends' keyword sets the parent class. Polymorphism lets a child class override parent methods with @Override.

exercises.question: Which keyword is used to inherit from a class in Java?

lessons.title: Collections: List & Map
lessons.content: Java Collections Framework provides List (ordered, allows duplicates) and Map (key-value pairs). ArrayList and HashMap are the most common implementations.

exercises.question: Which collection stores key-value pairs in Java?
hints: It maps keys to values, like a dictionary
